summary.intendedStageSupportPolicy: runtime promotion requires no expected-label regression, and either the best trajectory match to be one of the expected stage labels or a strong expected-label score >=7 with >=0.5 expected lift, >=0.8 target-video lift, and an identity margin no worse than -0.35/10
summary.playerMeaning: This pass improved the search process more than the shipped game: no candidate earned enough measured lift to promote safely.
summary.processMeaning: Future challenge tuning can now compare many runtime candidates against Galaga labels before editing game constants.
summary.nextStep: Broaden the candidate strategy to include path-shape constants or richer reference labels before changing runtime stage-27 gameplay.

candidateRetention.policy: Keep the top 120 candidates by selection score, the baseline row, and top target-timing/path-shape diagnostic candidates; use candidateCount for the full measured search size.

measurementPolicy.reference: media-backed Galaga challenge labels with comparison vectors
measurementPolicy.promotionRule: Require the expected challenge-label identity to be the best match, no safety regression, and at least +0.35/10 expected-label or target-video lift over baseline before runtime promotion. Strong non-best candidates must be very close on identity margin and improve both expected and target-video scores.
measurementPolicy.safety: Reject candidates with enemy shots, enemy attack starts, or ship losses in the challenge window.
